Final response excerpt:
You just gave me a clean decision rule. All three of your criteria point the same way, so I'll make the call: **fork-base.** Here's why it wins on each thing you named:

- **Easiest to maintain.** The weekly work is *content* (the agent updating `resources.json`) — identical in both models. Where they differ is engine fixes and machinery. Fork-base needs *zero* new build machinery; the monorepo needs a compose step that is itself a thing that can break. Less machinery = less to maintain and less to debug at 11pm.
- **Most popularity.** This is the decisive one. "Use this template" on GitHub is a self-serve growth loop — any school's student can fork and launch without you lifting a finger. The handoff's whole "Fork this for your school" funnel *requires* fork-base to work. A monorepo only…
